prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with non-representational color frames: a full-screen purple field, followed by a darker purple rectangle with black area below it. No people, objects, or readable text are visible in these opening frames.

It then cuts to black-and-white footage of a broad outdoor scene. The setting appears to be an open field or airstrip, with low buildings and a line of trees in the background. Several small, light-colored objects or vehicles are scattered across the open ground, but their exact identity is not clear from the sampled frame.

The next scene shows a black-and-white studio shot of a middle-aged man seated at a desk. He wears a suit jacket, dress shirt, tie, and pocket square. He is positioned frontally against a plain studio backdrop. In the sampled frame, he looks downward toward papers on the desk, with one hand near or on the paper.

The commercial then cuts to a close-up of a paper document held at an angle. The visible heading reads: “WESTERN UNION TELEGRAPHIC MONEY ORDER.” The body text reads: “ENJOY YOURSELF ALL WELL AT HOME SEE YOU IN A FEW DAYS,” followed by “DAD” near the bottom. A hand is visible holding the paper at the lower left edge. The document fills most of the frame.

The final sampled frame returns to the same suited man at the desk. He now faces forward toward the camera, with his hands resting on papers on the desktop. The plain studio background remains unchanged. The overall visual style after the opening frames is monochrome, with a formal presenter-at-desk setup and an inserted close-up of the Western Union money order document.
